name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: PRICES DOUBLED — KHARIF AT RISK: Global urea $720-800/ton (from $350 late 2025). Triple disruption: QAFCO offline (14% global, 5-year repair), Hormuz blocking 43% seaborne exports, India domestic plants at 70% capacity after Petronet force majeure. India stocks 61 LMT but drawdown exceeds replenishment. FY27 subsidy budget CUT despite price doubling — fiscal math broken. Kharif 2026 sowing (June-July) is the cliff: 65% of annual urea consumed in Kharif. Diversifying to China/Russia/Morocco but timeline tight.

current.snapshot_note: US Gulf urea nearly doubled from $350 late 2025 to $800+ by late March 2026

signals.content: QatarEnergy shut world's largest urea plant after Ras Laffan damage. Urea export prices surged ~40% from under $500 to over $700/mt almost immediately.
PM Modi in Lok Sabha assured farmers of fertilizer availability despite global price spikes. Government absorbing some cost through subsidies.

QatarEnergy expanded production suspension beyond LNG to include entire downstream: urea, methanol, polymers, and aluminum. QAFCO's 5.6 Mt/y Mesaieed urea plant — world's largest single-site urea facility — offline since March 4. Gulf producers account for 43% of all seaborne urea exports, now largely offline. US retail urea price at $674/ton (second week of March), up 12% MoM. New Orleans barge prices jumped from $475 to $520-550/ton in single week. CRU Group warns disruptions 'could be catastrophic' if prolonged. WEF published analysis stating crisis 'demonstrates the fragility of global food supply.' India's situation: government claims record pre-Kharif stocks (59-62 lakh tonnes urea) but LNG supply cuts from Qatar forced 30% feedstock reduction to domestic fertilizer plants — three urea plants reduced output. India sources 40%+ of urea from Middle East. Government quietly approached China for urea supply (BigStory Network) while publicly denying shortage. Imported urea subsidies expected to increase 52%+.

Urea at ~$640/mt (+12% MoM). India approaching China for emergency urea supply while publicly denying shortage. Domestic fertilizer plants at 70% capacity due to LNG feedstock cuts from Qatar disruption. European TTF surge (+70% in March to €54.52/MWh) directly increases fertilizer production costs globally — natural gas is primary feedstock for nitrogen-based fertilizers. Connected to food security cascade for India: Kharif 2026 planting season at risk if supply doesn't normalize.

COMPREHENSIVE UPDATE: Global urea prices surged from $350/ton (late 2025) to over $800/ton in US Gulf and $720+/ton globally — more than doubled in one month. Three layers of disruption compounding: (1) QAFCO offline since March 4 (14% of global urea, 5-year repair timeline), (2) Hormuz blocking 43% of seaborne urea exports, (3) India's domestic production hit by LNG feedstock cut — Petronet LNG invoked force majeure March 3, gas supply at ~70% of requirement, IFFCO and other plants shut or reduced output. India's stocks at 61.14 LMT (vs 55.22 last year) — looks adequate but drawdown rate exceeds replenishment. Government secured 23% more spot gas (32→39.31 MMSCMD) but at expensive TTF-linked prices. FY27 fertilizer subsidy budget: ₹1.70 lakh crore (DOWN from ₹1.86 lakh crore) — budgeted LESS while urea prices more than doubled. Import subsidy allocation ₹32,000 crore vs ₹52,000 crore last year. Either mid-year revision (fiscal deficit widens) or farmers bear cost. India diversifying to China, Russia, Morocco, Australia — 1.3M tons ordered but 'some may not arrive on time' (Bloomberg). Critical Kharif timeline: sowing June-July, 65% of annual urea used in Kharif. If stocks deplete by May, sowing area drops 10-15%. CF Industries surging as US nitrogen beneficiary. (Sources: Bloomberg, CNBC, ChemAnalyst, IMARC, Argus Media, Business Standard, CRU Group)

edges.context: Natural gas is primary input for nitrogen-based fertilizers. Gas price spike → fertilizer cost spike. India's domestic plants cut 30% feedstock.
QAFCO Mesaieed (world's largest single-site urea plant, 5.6 Mt/y) offline since Mar 4. Entire Qatar downstream complex shut.
India imports 40%+ of urea from Middle East. Domestic plants at 70% capacity due to LNG cuts. Quietly approaching China. Kharif 2026 planting season at risk.

trigger_points.condition: Urea price sustained above $800/mt for 4+ weeks
trigger_points.mechanism: Would strain India's fertilizer subsidy budget. Either government absorbs (fiscal deficit) or passes to farmers (food inflation). Political sensitivity high ahead of state elections.

trigger_points.condition: India urea stocks fall below 40 LMT by May 1, 2026
trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal Kharif 2026 sowing area at serious risk of 10-15% reduction. Food inflation +5-8pp by Q4 FY27. Forces RBI to hold/hike rates instead of cutting. Political crisis for government ahead of state elections.
